title: TAC-459: Generative AI and Natural Language Processing
overview: Welcome to TAC 459 - Generative AI and Natural Language Processing (NLP) at the University of Southern California. This course is designed to immerse students in the rapidly evolving fields of Generative AI and NLP, providing hands-on experience with cutting-edge Machine Learning Techniques & LLMs. Throughout the semester, you'll explore how generative models are transforming industries, from text generation to conversational agents. Students will gain hands-on experience with tools such as Hugging Face, TensorFlow, and PyTorch, while learning about applications in conversational agents, text summarization, and machine translation. By the end, students will be prepared to tackle real-world challenges in AI-driven fields.

instructors.title: Instructor
instructors.name: Prof. Allen Bolourchi
instructors.bio: Professor Allen Bolourchi is a visionary leader in generative AI and natural language processing with an extensive career spanning academia, research, and industry. As an adjunct professor at the University of Southern California, he educates future AI professionals on cutting-edge generative AI techniques. Simultaneously, Allen plays a pivotal role in AI research and product management at Meta AI, FAIR. His prior experience includes strategic positions at Apple, where he managed machine learning initiatives for the App Store, and leadership roles in product development. Recognized for his contributions with numerous fellowships and awards, Allen’s blend of academic rigor and industry expertise drives innovation and transformational growth in technology.

instructors.title: Instructor
instructors.name: Prof. Reza Jafarkhani
instructors.bio: Reza Jafarkhani is an accomplished academic and industry professional specializing in machine learning, artificial intelligence, and data analytics. Currently a Senior Lecturer at the University of Southern California’s Viterbi School of Engineering, he brings over five years of teaching experience in advanced technical subjects including AI and programming. He is dedicated to bridging theoretical knowledge with real-world applications, preparing the next generation of engineers for emerging technological challenges.

teachingAssistants.title: Course Assistant
teachingAssistants.name: Rajeev Singh
teachingAssistants.bio: Rajeev Singh is a passionate software engineer with a robust background in full-stack development, cloud computing, and machine learning. Currently pursuing his Master’s in Computer Science at the University of Southern California, Rajeev has honed his skills through practical experiences that include designing CI/CD pipelines and building real-time applications. His tenure at companies such as ASML, where he enhanced system uptime and error resolution efficiency, highlights his ability to develop scalable systems and optimize infrastructure. Rajeev will be joining Meta as a Software Engineer from June 2025

teachingAssistants.title: Course Assistant
teachingAssistants.name: Nitin Sai Bommi
teachingAssistants.bio: Nitin Sai Bommi is an emerging expert in deep learning and machine learning with a solid research and academic foundation. Having earned his Master’s degree from the University of Hyderabad—with a thesis focused on Explainable AI for Software Engineering—he is now advancing his studies at the University of Southern California. Nitin has built a diverse portfolio through internships at NVIDIA, where he is involved in deep learning performance analysis, as well as research roles at the University of Calgary, University College Dublin, and the University of Hyderabad. His proficiency in Python and machine learning, coupled with his hands-on experience in AI profiling and performance optimization, marks him as a promising innovator in the field. Nitin will be joining NVIDIA in May 2025 after his graduation.​

teachingAssistants.title: Course Assistant
teachingAssistants.name: Varuna Krishna Kolla
teachingAssistants.bio: Varuna Krishna is a driven computer science professional with extensive experience in machine learning and artificial intelligence. Currently pursuing his Master’s in Computer Science at the University of Southern California, he also serves as a Teaching Assistant for the Applied Machine Learning for Natural Language Processing course. His professional journey includes impactful roles such as a Generative AI Engineer Intern at Vimaan, Machine Learning Intern at AutomationEdge, and a research internship at the University of South Carolina’s Artificial Intelligence Institute. Varuna’s work on projects like Image2Tweet and innovative joint embedding learning techniques underlines his commitment to advancing AI. With robust programming skills in C, HTML, and Python, he is well-equipped to tackle complex technical challenges and contribute to the tech industry. Varuna will be joining Amazon as a Software Developer from June 2025.

link.text: Email Professor atleast 48 hours before to Schedule(15 minutes meet)

courseSchedule.title: Generative AI, Products, Techniques, APIs, and Ethical Considerations
topics: Generative AI, Products, Techniques, APIs, and Ethical Considerations
Generative Models in NLP (ChatGPT, BARD, Gemini, Llama)
Advanced Prompt Engineering: Few-shot, Chain-of-thought, and Self-Consistency
Expanding LLM Capabilities: Knowledge Generation Prompting and Program-aided (PAL)
Developing an API with MongoDB and ngrok, and interfacing via Postman
LLM Safety: Prompt Injection, Prompt Leaking, Jailbreaking
Responsible AI, Data Privacy, Ethical Considerations, and Governance in AI
Business Intelligence, Marketing, Analytics, and Brand Analysis Use Cases
Introduction to Product Management and Business Considerations

courseSchedule.title: Training and Fine-Tuning LLMs, Hugging Face, LangChain, and RAG
topics: Training and Fine-Tuning LLMs, Hugging Face, LangChain, and RAG
Training of Chat GPT with Reinforcement Learning, HITL, and Proximal Policy Optimization

LoRA and QLoRA for Efficient Model Adaptation
Fine Tuning LLMs, Best Practices and Challenges
Overview and Practical Use of Hugging Face in NLP Based Products
Utilizing LangChain and Llama for Customized Language Models
